# L2PS Feature Develop - Multi-Agent Pipeline

A self-contained, iterative, multi-agent development pipeline for the Nokia 5G U-Plane **L2-PS** component. Given **one feature** as input - which may contain **multiple functional points (FPs)** with optional declared dependencies - the pipeline analyses the dependency graph between FPs and fixes a cross-FP **blueprint** (a frozen contract for naming, shared types, error model, hot-path rules, interface ownership, and extension points) in a single **FEATURE PLAN** stage, then develops each FP end-to-end as its own local commit:

```
spec intake (pipeline-internal)
            -> feature planner (DAG + topo sort + cross-FP blueprint)
            -> per-FP loop, in topological order:
                 per-FP architect -> arch reviewer (design audit, pre-code)
                                  -> developer -> UT tester
                                  -> SCT tester -> reviewer (robustness + code review)
                                  -> commit (pipeline-internal)
            -> MERGE-READY (one local commit per FP)
```

Two phases are performed **inline by the pipeline agent itself** — Spec Intake (feature-level) and Commit composition (per FP). They are routine and integrate tightly with the pipeline's git hygiene and persistence; a separate sub-agent would only add a round-trip without adding value. All other stages still delegate to specialist sub-agents.

**One pipeline invocation = one feature = N local commits**, where `N` is the number of FPs in the feature. The pipeline iterates with a bounded negotiation loop **per FP** (max 3 cycles per FP) until each FP is merge-ready or the user is asked for guidance.

<a id="authoring-specs"></a>
## Authoring feature markdown (human writers)

Keep the **input markdown** to **product content only**:

- **Mandatory:** a **Feature ID** line near the top, e.g. `Feature ID: CB013943` or `Feature ID: CNI12345` (alphanumeric product key used for SCT directory layout and traceability). Optional: `Subfeature ID: CB013943-A`.
- **Include:** feature overview, targets, functional points (`FPn`) with trigger / behaviour / acceptance, optional explicit `Depends on: FPx, FPy` (or `none`), and normative algorithms or O&M / signalling text that belongs in the requirement.
- **Do not include:** per-stage agent instructions, status-report templates, "expected deliverables" checklists, implementation-style guidance (hot-path numerics, framework bans, progress tracking formats). Those belong in the **Pipeline agent** and other stage agent definitions (Spec Intake inline phase, Architect, Developer, testers, Reviewer) — see `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` (for Spec Intake) and `l2ps-feature-developer.agent.md`.

The pipeline agent owns **per-FP progress** (status table, cycles, commits). Authors do not need to embed progress blocks in the spec file.

<a id="scope"></a>
## Scope

This pipeline strictly targets the L2-PS U-Plane component:

- Production C++ source: `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/src/`
- Interface files used by L2-PS: `/workspace/itf/`
- Unit tests: `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/src/<comp>/ut/`
- FUSE SCT for L2-PS: `/workspace/uplane/sct/cpp_testsuites/fuse/testEnvironments/l2ps/`

It must **not** be used for the C-Plane. C-Plane work is owned by different agents and is explicitly out-of-scope here.

**L1 interface headers** under `/workspace/uplane/sdkuplane/prefix-root/include/interfaces-l1/` are generated by a separate multi-repo and are normally read-only for this pipeline. A narrow **local-coding workaround** is permitted when the currently-checked-in generated headers do not yet expose what an FP needs; it is scoped to the L2-PS-facing subtrees of the four L2-PS-related packages (`NR_L1DL_L2PS/L2_PS/`, `NR_L1UL_L2PS/L2_PS/`, `NR_L1DLPOOL_L2PS/L2_PS_5G/`, `NR_L1ULPOOL_L2PS/L2_PS_5G/`), and authorisation lives in the per-FP architect plan. The authoritative policy is in `l2ps-feature-architect.agent.md` → `## L1 interface workaround (local-only)`; downstream gates live in the Developer and Reviewer agents, and in the pipeline-internal Commit step's pre-commit checks.

<a id="repo-layout"></a>
## Repository layout

| Item | Path |
|------|------|
| gNB repo root | `/workspace` |
| L2-PS production source | `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/src/` |
| L2-PS unit tests | `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/src/<comp>/ut/` |
| FUSE SCT (host) for L2-PS | `/workspace/uplane/sct/cpp_testsuites/fuse/testEnvironments/l2ps/` |
| Interface files (`.mt`) | `/workspace/itf/` |
| Robustness rules | `/workspace/.github/rules/review/uplane/` |
| Repo entry agent doc | `/workspace/AGENTS.md` |
| U-Plane routing agent doc | `/workspace/uplane/AGENTS.md` |
| L2-PS agent doc | `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/AGENTS.md` |
| FUSE agent doc | `/workspace/uplane/sct/cpp_testsuites/fuse/AGENTS.md` |

This pack lives wherever the user installs it (the canonical default is `${HOME}/work/doc/ai/.github/agents/`, but **any** VS Code workspace root containing `.github/agents/l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` works — the pipeline agent self-locates at preflight). Flat layout: every `*.agent.md` plus this `README.md` sit directly under `.github/agents/`. The bundle helper `scripts/make-bundle.sh` lives one level up at the pack root (shared with the parallel `.github/agents/` copy); the pipeline agent locates it via Glob.

<a id="agent-files"></a>
## Agent files in this directory

| Stage | File / performer | User-invocable | Permissions | Runs |
|-------|------------------|----------------|-------------|------|
| - | `README.md` (this file) | n/a | n/a | n/a |
| Pipeline (entry) | `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` | yes | read, search, edit, execute, todo | 1x |
| 0. Intake | **pipeline-internal** (see `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` → *Spec Intake*) | n/a | inherits pipeline's | 1x |
| 1. Planner (DAG + blueprint) | `l2ps-feature-planner.agent.md` | no | read, search, todo | 1x |
| 2. per-FP Architect | `l2ps-feature-architect.agent.md` | no | read, search, todo | 1x per FP (plus arch-review loop-backs) |
| 2r. per-FP Arch Reviewer | `l2ps-feature-arch-reviewer.agent.md` | no | read, search, todo | 1x per FP (plus loop-backs) |
| 3. Developer | `l2ps-feature-developer.agent.md` | no | read, search, edit, execute, todo | 1x per FP |
| 4. UT Tester | `l2ps-feature-ut-tester.agent.md` | no | read, search, edit, execute, todo | 1x per FP |
| 5. SCT Tester | `l2ps-feature-sct-tester.agent.md` | no | read, search, edit, execute, todo | 1x per FP |
| 6. Reviewer (robustness + code review) | `l2ps-feature-reviewer.agent.md` | no | read, search, agent, todo | 1x per FP |
| 7. Commit | **pipeline-internal** (see `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` → *Commit composition*) | n/a | inherits pipeline's | 1x per FP |
| 8. Bundle | **pipeline-internal** (see `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` → *Bundle composition*) | n/a | inherits pipeline's | 1x per run |
| - | `scripts/make-bundle.sh` (helper invoked by stage 8) | n/a | n/a | n/a |

Stages 0 (Intake), 7 (Commit), and 8 (Bundle) are performed **inline by the pipeline agent** — they are routine spec normalization, git plumbing, and post-run archiving that already require the pipeline's own permissions. The remaining stages (1, 2, 2r, 3-6) are sub-agents. Stage 1 (Planner) runs **once** for the whole feature; its output (the FEATURE PLAN) contains both the dependency DAG / topological order **and** the cross-FP blueprint. Stages 2, 2r, and 3-6 run **once per FP** (plus loop-backs), in the topological order produced by the Planner; every per-FP stage receives the (frozen) FEATURE PLAN as read-only input. Stage 2r (Arch Reviewer) audits the per-FP Architect's design plan **before any code is written** — design defects loop straight back to the Architect, never reaching the (more expensive) Developer / UT / SCT stages. Stage 6 (post-implementation Reviewer) folds in the U-Plane robustness rule-set scan over the FP's changed files; there is no separate Robustness stage. Stage 8 (Bundle) runs **once per terminal state** (success or unrecoverable escalation), enriches `RUN_ROOT` in place (`README.md` + `MANIFEST.json` + `code-changes/`), and produces a sibling tarball `${RUN_ROOT}.tar.gz`. If preflight could not locate `scripts/make-bundle.sh` in any VS Code workspace path, stage 8 is skipped with a one-line note.

<a id="entry-point"></a>
## How to invoke (pipeline agent)

The only user-invocable agent is the pipeline agent.

**One invocation = one feature = N local commits** (one per FP), **except** <a href="#entry-point">Mode 3 — `RUN_ROOT check`</a> which is a read-only audit of an existing run directory.

**Run logs:** the pipeline agent persists each stage agent's full reply (plus escalations and the final summary) under `${RUN_ROOT} = ~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/<FeatureId>/<SubFeatureId>-<RunStamp>/` when file tools are enabled. `RUNS_BASE = ~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/` is **hardcoded** (no override) — it lives outside both `/workspace/` and this pack repo, so `git status` stays clean in both. Per-FP outputs go into `${RUN_ROOT}/FP<FPId>/` subdirectories (where `FPId` is the lowercase-letter identifier `a`/`b`/`c`/... that aligns with SCT-case naming). Every persisted filename carries the `<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>[-FP<FPId>]` prefix so a file is unambiguous even when copied out of `RUN_ROOT`. See <a href="#faq-reports">FAQ: Stage reports persistence</a>.

**Deliverable bundle:** at the end of every run (success, partial, or escalated), stage 8 **enriches `RUN_ROOT` in place** — adding `README.md` (status header), `MANIFEST.json` (machine-readable metadata), and `code-changes/` (a `git format-patch` of all FP commits plus before/after snapshots of every changed file — run `diff -ruN before/ after/` for a complete code-review-ready diff). A sibling tarball `${RUN_ROOT}.tar.gz` is produced for offline sharing. If preflight could not locate `scripts/make-bundle.sh`, stage 8 is skipped with a one-line note (RUN_ROOT remains the authoritative record either way). See <a href="#faq-bundle">FAQ: Deliverable bundle</a>.

A feature may include multiple functional points. They are analysed for dependencies, topologically sorted, then each one is developed end-to-end (architect -> ... -> commit) on its own in dependency order. Each FP that passes review produces its **own** local commit.

### Mode 1: markdown spec file (recommended)

Write the feature spec (with its functional points) in a markdown file, then invoke:

```
@l2ps-feature-pipeline path/to/feature.md
@l2ps-feature-pipeline /workspace/uplane/L2-PS/doc/mcs-cap.md
@l2ps-feature-pipeline ./local-spec.md
```

The pipeline agent reads the file once, performs the inline Spec Intake (extracts the FP list and any explicit `Depends on:` annotations), lets the Planner compute the execution order (and the cross-FP blueprint), then runs the **per-FP 6-stage inner pipeline** (Architect → Arch Reviewer → Developer → UT → SCT → Reviewer) followed by the inline **Commit** step once for each FP in that order.

### Mode 2: inline spec

Paste the spec text directly:

```
@l2ps-feature-pipeline Add MCS cap enforcement on DL grants, plus ...
```

Same behaviour; the pipeline agent extracts FPs directly from the inline text.

### Mode 3: post-run `RUN_ROOT check` (read-only audit)

After a run has persisted logs under `~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/...`, re-invoke the **same** pipeline agent with:

```
@l2ps-feature-pipeline RUN_ROOT check ~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/CB013943/B-2026-05-27T143200Z
@l2ps-feature-pipeline check ~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/CB013943/B-2026-05-27T143200Z
```

Shorthand `check <dir>` is accepted **only** when `<dir>` is a directory that already contains pipeline `000-*-run-meta.md` (otherwise you get a one-line error, not INLINE). Long form `RUN_ROOT check` stays unambiguous if the first line of a spec could start with the word “check”.

This does **not** start Spec Intake, Planner, per-FP stages, commits, or bundle. It reads the existing `RUN_ROOT` (run-meta, manifest, design doc / final summary / Reviewer artifacts, etc.), emits a structured gap report in chat, optionally appends `{SEQ}-<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>-run-root-check.md` to that directory, and ends with `=== RUN_ROOT CHECK COMPLETE ===`. Add `no persist` / `chat only` on the same line to skip writing the extra file. Canonical rules: `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` → *RUN_ROOT check*.

### Recommended markdown format

The pipeline agent is tolerant about how FPs are delimited; pick the style that matches your spec. The key rule is: **make the FP boundaries clear**, then optionally state dependencies.

**A. Sub-headings (clearest for multi-point features):**

```markdown
# Feature: DL Scheduler MCS-cap suite
Feature ID: CB013943
Subfeature ID: CB013943-A
Tracking: FPB-251671

Overview: The DL scheduler should enforce a configurable MCS
cap, with separate behaviour for ...

## FP1: Enforce MCS cap per cell
Trigger: ...
Behaviour: ...
Acceptance: ...

## FP2: Per-UE override via JSON knob
Depends on: FP1
Trigger: ...
Behaviour: ...
Acceptance: ...

## FP3: Counter & tracing for capped grants
Depends on: FP1, FP2
Trigger: ...
Behaviour: ...
Acceptance: ...
```

**B. Top-level list of points:**

```markdown
# Feature: DL Scheduler MCS-cap suite (FPB-251671)

Overview: ...

Functional points:
- FP1: enforce MCS cap per cell
  Trigger: ...
  Behaviour: ...
- FP2: per-UE override via JSON knob
  Depends on: FP1
  ...
- FP3: counter & tracing for capped grants
  Depends on: FP1, FP2
  ...
```

**C. Prose with inline enumerators:** any prose like "the feature consists of (1) ..., (2) ..., (3) ...". The pipeline agent splits points at the enumerators. Dependencies must be declared with explicit `Depends on: FPx` lines if you use this style.

**D. Single-point spec:** any prose without enumeration. The pipeline agent treats the whole input as `FP1`. The Planner trivially returns `[FP1]` (with a trivial blueprint) and the inner pipeline runs exactly once.

Any `FPB-XXXXXX` / `PRXXXXXX` tracking ID anywhere in the spec is captured as a feature-level tracking ID and used by every FP's commit message.

### What you get back

- Status lines per stage, `MERGE-READY` per FP, final `=== FEATURE COMPLETE ===`, or `=== PIPELINE ESCALATION ===` when blocked.
- Internal agents are not invoked directly; the pipeline agent sequences them.

<a id="dependency-syntax"></a>
## Declaring dependencies between FPs

The Planner combines two dependency sources:

1. **Explicit declarations** that you write in the spec. These are forwarded verbatim through Spec Intake to the Planner. Any of the following shapes is accepted, anywhere inside an FP's body:

   ```
   Depends on: FP1
   Depends on: FP1, FP3
   Depends-on: FP1
   Deps: FP1, FP3
   ```

2. **Inferred dependencies** that the Planner derives by reading each FP's description and lightly scanning the L2-PS code for symbols / config knobs / behaviours referenced. For example, if `FP2` "calls `applyOverride()` newly introduced by `FP1`", the Planner adds the implicit edge `FP1 -> FP2` even if you did not write `Depends on:`.

Rules:

- An FP with **no** prior-FP dependencies has either no `Depends on:` line or `Depends on: none`.
- The Planner refuses cycles. If `FP1 -> FP2 -> FP1`, the pipeline escalates immediately and the user must fix the spec.
- For per-FP commits to be useful, design FPs so they are as independent as possible. Cross-FP shared files are fine (later FPs simply rebase their diff on prior commits) but cross-FP API churn should ideally itself be its own FP.
- Among FPs with no dependencies (i.e. zero in-degree), the Planner picks a deterministic order (smallest FP id first).

The Planner's `=== FEATURE PLAN ===` output shows the full DAG, the topological order, a per-FP justification for every dependency edge, and (in Part 2) the cross-FP blueprint. Review it before letting the inner pipeline run.

<a id="blueprint"></a>
## Feature blueprint (cross-FP contract inside the Plan)

The cross-FP contract (shared types, naming, error model, `.mt` ownership, hot-path rules, AC mapping) is **Part 2 of the FEATURE PLAN** emitted by `@l2ps-feature-planner` in stage 1. There is no separate blueprint stage. Every per-FP handoff embeds the full FEATURE PLAN block read-only; the per-FP Architect must include a `## Blueprint compliance` section in its design plan, and the Reviewer must verify it. Escalation / `amend plan` semantics are in `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` (see **Feature Plan lifecycle** and **Escalation protocol**).

<a id="external-deps"></a>
## External knowledge sources

Agents in this pipeline may read and follow the following files (read-only references; the pipeline does **not** modify them):

| File / Path | Purpose | Used by |
|-------------|---------|---------|
| `<pack-root>/storage/L2PS_Architecture.md` (`L2PS_ARCH_REF`) | **Pack-local L2-PS architecture knowledge base** (FR1 only; ~870 lines; code-anchored). Covers System Context, EO catalog, Flows, Database Architecture, Real-Time, Source Tree. Anchor for design decisions. Located at preflight by Glob-searching VS Code workspace paths for `**/storage/L2PS_Architecture.md`; absolute path passed through handoffs as `L2PS_ARCH_REF:` (or `none` if not found — agents proceed without it) | Planner, Architect, Arch Reviewer (primary); Developer / Reviewer / SCT Tester (optional, on architectural questions) |
| **gNB-side agents / skills** at `/workspace/.github/{agents,skills}/` (e.g. `L2PS-coding`, `L2PS-ut`, `local-smith`, `i-faster`, `commit-message-rules`, `l2ps-fuse-sct`, `l2ps-ut-generate`) | VS Code / GitHub Copilot auto-loads these by slug; pack agents reference them by name, no path lookup needed | All stages, as relevant |
| `/workspace/AGENTS.md` | Repo-wide entry | All stages |
| `/workspace/uplane/AGENTS.md` | U-Plane routing | All stages |
| `/workspace/uplane/L2-PS/AGENTS.md` | L2-PS scoping | All stages |
| `/workspace/uplane/sct/cpp_testsuites/fuse/AGENTS.md` | FUSE scoping | SCT Tester |
| `/workspace/.github/rules/review/uplane/*.rules.md` | Robustness rule files | Reviewer (robustness pass; may delegate to `@local-smith`) |
| `/workspace/uplane/.agents/skills/uplane-setup-SDK/SKILL.md` | SDK setup (host) | Developer (first-run) |

**Strict exclusions:** Do **not** read, reference, or follow any agent, skill, or rule scoped to the C-Plane. Examples to avoid: `cplane-*`, `cprt-*`, `cpcl-*`, `/workspace/cplane/`, C-Plane agent files under `/workspace/.github/agents/`.

<a id="non-goals"></a>
## Non-goals / out-of-scope

- Pushing commits to Gerrit or any remote. The pipeline stops at local commits (one per FP).
- Modifying `AGENTS.md` files in `/workspace`.
- C-Plane changes.
- L2-HI changes (the FUSE notes in `uplane/AGENTS.md` confirm that L2-HI is not tested with FUSE here).
- Target-board (on-target) builds. Pipeline targets host builds only. On-target verification, if required, is escalated.
- Reordering or rewriting previously committed FPs within the same pipeline run. If a downstream FP discovers its dependency was wrong, the pipeline escalates - the user must decide whether to drop, abort, or re-invoke with a corrected spec.

<a id="format-rules"></a>
## Format rules for editing the agent files

These rules are the self-contained formatting standard for **all** files in this directory. They replace any C-Plane format reference used by predecessor pipelines.

1. **YAML front-matter first.** Required keys: `name`, `description`, `argument-hint`, `tools`, `user-invocable`, `disable-model-invocation`. Optional comments may follow.
2. **Prose layout:** keep one logical paragraph per block (no manual hard-wrap at a column width). Use blank lines between paragraphs; let the editor soft-wrap. Tables, fenced code, and copy-paste templates are exempt.
3. **Headings:** `#` for the title (matching `name:`), `##` for sections. Mirror the order of the Table of Contents.
4. **Table of Contents:** every agent file MUST have a `## Table of Contents` near the top, with HTML anchors above each section.
5. **Mandatory sections** in every agent file, in this order:
   1. YAML front-matter
   2. Table of Contents
   3. `# <Agent Name>` (purpose)
   4. `## Mandatory instructions for the AI`
   5. `## Scope and constraints`
   6. `## Workflow`
   7. Stage-specific sections (commands, conventions, etc.)
   8. `## Output format`
6. **Last line discipline:** every response from an agent must end with a literal line:

   ```
   Used Agent: **<Agent Name>**
   ```

7. **No code-narration comments** in code snippets - only non-obvious intent.
8. **English only.** No non-English text in agent files.
9. **No C-Plane references.** See exclusions above.
10. **Self-reference for format updates.** When editing any file in this directory, follow these rules; do not refer to any C-Plane format file.

<a id="orchestration"></a>
## Orchestration details (header, routing, cycle counter)

The **single source of truth** for the per-FP inner loop lives in `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` — concretely in these sections:

| Topic | Authoritative section in `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` |
|-------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| Pipeline header block stage agents parse on every handoff | *Handoff message format* (anchor `#handoff`) |
| Stage-by-stage gate conditions inside an FP | *Pipeline coordination rules* → Rule O-3 |
| Loop-back routing matrix (per failure type → restart stage / re-run sequence / `Open scope`) | *Loop-back routing (within a single FP)* (anchor `#routing`) |
| Cycle counter semantics (per FP, exceptions, resume reset) | *Pipeline coordination rules* → Rule O-7 |
| Chat-resume / "continue" discipline (delegation must survive a resume) | *Pipeline coordination rules* → Rule O-16 |

This README does **not** duplicate those tables — they live in exactly one place so a rule change does not have to be edited twice. If you need to read them as a stage author, follow the link.

**Granularity principle (applies to all loop-backs):** never re-run downstream stages that were not invalidated by the loop-back fix. Only the pipeline agent decides re-run scope; stage agents must honour the `Open scope` field on the handoff.

**Inter-FP failures** (an already-committed dependency was wrong) escalate immediately — they are never auto-fixed.

**Blueprint failures never auto-loop-back.** Any `BLUEPRINT` issue (from the per-FP Architect, Arch Reviewer, Developer, or post-implementation Reviewer) escalates to the user, who chooses: `amend plan` (re-run stage 1 once, then resume the current FP at `Cycle = 0 (resume)` — already-committed FPs are NOT replayed), rework the FP under the existing Plan, drop the FP, or abort. An `OPEN_QUESTION` finding from the Arch Reviewer escalates the same way: only the user can clarify product-level intent the Architect lacked.

<a id="hands-free"></a>
## Hands-free (unattended) run

Markdown agents cannot disable VS Code / Copilot's global safety UI. For a **single uninterrupted run**:

1. **Session settings:** allow the Agent to run terminal and file tools **without** approving every command (use the product's auto-run / YOLO-equivalent mode for this session only).
2. **pipeline agent behaviour:** already avoids asking for `continue` / `resume` between stages (Rule O-6); it only pauses on a formal `=== PIPELINE ESCALATION ===` block or the dedicated `=== PIPELINE SCT-SKIP HANDSHAKE ===` mini-block — see the pipeline agent's *Escalation protocol*. Everything else (build errors, test failures, robustness findings, reviewer changes-required) loops back internally.
3. **Commands:** stage agents must use non-interactive flags (`-y`, `--no-pager`, etc.) and never block on an open pager.

### What happens if the IDE shows "Continue?" mid-run

VS Code / Copilot Chat may pause the chat when the AI hits its per-turn token / step budget, prompting the user to click *Continue*. This is **not** an escalation and **not** a user product decision — it is a chat-level resume. The pipeline agent's Rule O-16 (*Continue / chat-resume discipline*) handles this:

- On resume, the agent's first action is to re-identify the active stage from the latest persisted run-log file (`RUN_ROOT/<largest SEQ>...md`).
- If a subagent was running when the chat broke, the pipeline agent **re-invokes that same subagent** with the same handoff block marked `Resume mode: resume`. It does NOT inline-perform the subagent's work — that would be a Rule O-1 / O-2 violation.
- The pipeline agent emits `[Pipeline] Resume: re-delegating to <STAGE_NAME> for FP <FPid>` so the delegation is visible in the UI.

If you see the pipeline appear to "do everything in the main agent" after clicking Continue, that is a Rule O-16 violation — file it as a bug against the pipeline agent. The fix is in the agent's behaviour, not in user actions.

### The only sanctioned pauses

The pipeline pauses only for:

- A `=== PIPELINE ESCALATION ===` block (production decisions and unrecoverable failures — see *Escalation protocol* in the pipeline agent for the full list).
- A `=== PIPELINE SCT-SKIP HANDSHAKE ===` block (when an SCT Tester reports `NEED_USER_CONFIRMATION` for a Tier-C FP — see the SCT Tester's *Mandatory SCT coverage policy*). Reply `confirm skip sct` or `design sct anyway`.

If the IDE still blocks on a specific tool outside these two paths, that is outside this pack's control — fix the tool policy once, then re-invoke the pipeline agent.

### Design document (terminal run only)

When the run ends with `=== FEATURE COMPLETE ===` (`ALL MERGE-READY`, `PARTIAL`, or `NONE`), the pipeline agent **first** persists `{SEQ}-<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>-design-document.md` at the top of `RUN_ROOT` — one markdown file that rolls up the normalized spec, the frozen FEATURE PLAN, each **committed** FP's last Architect Form A (pre-commit gate), and a commit-pointer table. It is written **without** user interaction (same hands-free session as the rest of the terminal pass) and is picked up automatically by the Stage 8 tarball. Full assembly rules live in the pipeline agent under *Design document (pipeline-internal)*.

<a id="faq-reports"></a>
## FAQ: Are intermediate reports (e.g. robustness) saved?

**Yes — when you run the pipeline agent with file tools enabled**, it MUST persist stage outputs under `${RUN_ROOT} = ${RUNS_BASE}/<FeatureId>/<SubFeatureId>-<RunStamp>/`. `RUNS_BASE = ~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/` is **hardcoded** (no override). Per-FP stage outputs go into `${RUN_ROOT}/FP<FPId>/` subdirectories (`FPId` = lowercase letter `a`/`b`/`c`/...); cross-FP outputs (Spec Intake, Planner, design document, final summary, Bundle stamp) sit flat at the top of `RUN_ROOT`. Every filename carries an identifier prefix — `<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>` for cross-FP files, `<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>-FP<FPId>` for per-FP files — so an artifact is unambiguous when copied out of context. Example per-FP path: `${RUN_ROOT}/FPa/006-CB013943-B-FPa-stage-03-developer__c0.md`. The canonical naming convention is documented in the pipeline agent's *Report persistence* section.

Co-locating the run logs with the pack — while keeping them out of git — gives:

- `git status` stays clean in **both** this pack repo (gitignored) and the gNB repo at `/workspace` (run logs are never written there).
- Run logs sit next to the agent files that produced them, so audits / postmortems are one `cd` away.
- Run logs survive `git clean` / fresh checkouts of the gNB repo, since they live inside the pack repo's working tree.

See **Report persistence** in `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` for naming (`SEQ`, YAML front matter), escalation / final-summary files, and optional `MANIFEST.md`. Robustness findings live inside the Reviewer artifact (`stage-06-reviewer__c<C>.md`) rather than a separate file.

What **else** is persisted besides `RUN_ROOT`:

- **Git commits** (one per FP) and the diffs they contain — these live in the gNB repo, not under `RUN_ROOT`.
- Anything **explicitly written to disk** by a stage agent (code, tests, SCT files) per its scope — also in the gNB repo.
- The **deliverable bundle** — produced by enriching `RUN_ROOT` itself (added `README.md` / `MANIFEST.json` / `code-changes/`) plus a sibling tarball `${RUN_ROOT}.tar.gz` — see the next FAQ.

<a id="faq-bundle"></a>
## FAQ: Deliverable bundle

**What is it?** At the end of every pipeline run, stage 8 (Bundle composition, pipeline-internal) **enriches `RUN_ROOT` in place** (adding `README.md` / `MANIFEST.json` / `code-changes/`) and produces a sibling tarball `${RUN_ROOT}.tar.gz`. The enriched RUN_ROOT + tarball are the deliverable; they are **redundant** with the in-tree stage artifacts + git history by design — a portable snapshot for review handoff / postmortem / offline diffing.

**Where does it go?** Directly into `RUN_ROOT` itself: `~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/<FeatureId>/<SubFeatureId>-<RunStamp>/` (enrichment files at the top; existing run logs untouched) plus a sibling tarball at `~/Downloads/l2ps-feature-runs/<FeatureId>/<SubFeatureId>-<RunStamp>.tar.gz`. Path is fixed (no override) — to relocate the whole run-log home, change `RUNS_BASE` (currently hardcoded in the pipeline agent).

**When does it run?** On **every** terminal state of the pipeline:

| Terminal state | Bundle `PIPELINE_STATUS` field |
|----------------|--------------------------------|
| All FPs `COMMITTED` | `ALL_MERGE_READY` |
| Some `COMMITTED`, some `DROPPED` / `BLOCKED` | `PARTIAL` |
| Zero `COMMITTED` | `NONE` |
| Pipeline emits an escalation block and waits for user | `ESCALATED` |
| User typed `abort` | `ABANDONED` |

The SCT-skip mini-handshake is **not** terminal; it does not trigger a bundle.

**What does it contain?**

| Sub-path | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `README.md` | Human-readable header (status, SHAs, commit count, inspection commands). |
| `MANIFEST.json` | Machine-readable summary (`schema: l2ps-feature-bundle/v1`). |
| `01-input/` | Original user spec (FILE mode) + Spec Intake `normalized-spec.md`. |
| `03-code-changes/` | `changes.patch` (`git format-patch BASE..HEAD`), `changes-stat.txt`, `commits.md`, plus **`before/`** + **`after/`** snapshots of every changed file in their natural directory layout. |
| `04-feature-plan/` | The latest Planner artifact (FEATURE PLAN, including any amendments). |
| `05-summary/` | The FINAL summary block. |

**How do I inspect the code changes?**

```bash
cd ${RUN_ROOT}
diff -ruN 03-code-changes/before/ 03-code-changes/after/   # full review-ready diff
less 03-code-changes/commits.md                            # commit subjects + bodies
git -C /workspace am < 03-code-changes/changes.patch       # replay onto BASE_SHA
```

**Is the bundle script reusable?** Yes. The helper at `scripts/make-bundle.sh` reads its inputs from environment variables (`RUN_ROOT`, `FEATURE_ID`, `SUBFEATURE_ID`, `RUN_STAMP`, `BASE_SHA`, `HEAD_SHA`, `PIPELINE_STATUS`, optional `INPUT_SPEC_PATH`, `GNB_REPO`); you can re-run it manually using the values recorded in `${RUN_ROOT}/000-<FeatureId>-<SubFeatureId>-run-meta.md`.

**Does the bundle ever pollute git?** No. `RUN_ROOT` lives under `~/Downloads/`, outside both this pack repo and the gNB repo at `/workspace`. Both `git status` outputs stay clean regardless of how many runs accumulate. The pipeline agent emits a one-line `[Pipeline] Retention: ...` warning when ≥ 10 `<SubFeatureId>-<RunStamp>/` directories accumulate under `${RUNS_BASE}/*/`, but never auto-deletes anything — pruning is up to you.

<a id="user-vs-auto"></a>
## User-invocable vs internal agents

Only `l2ps-feature-pipeline.agent.md` is marked `user-invocable: true`. All others are `user-invocable: false` and `disable-model-invocation: false` (so the pipeline agent can route to them automatically) but they should not be invoked by humans except for debugging.
